perturbation.original_text: At the written request of the University or upon expiry or earlier termination of this Agreement, Recipient will, on the direction of the University, return or destroy the Information and will not retain any photocopy or other reproduction of any part of the Information.
perturbation.changed_text: At the written request of the University, the Recipient may, at their sole discretion, return or destroy the Information. The Recipient may retain any photocopy or other reproduction of any part of the Information indefinitely.
perturbation.explanation: Changing Section 7 to say the Recipient *may* at their discretion return or destroy information, completely reverses the original text, which stated the Recipient *will* return or destroy the information at the direction of the University. It also adds that the Recipient *may* retain any copies, which also contradicts the original text which stated the Recipient will not retain any copies.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. Return or Destruction of Information. At the written request of the University, the Recipient may, at their sole discretion, return or destroy the Information. The Recipient may retain any photocopy or other reproduction of any part of the Information indefinitely.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original text and the contradicted text. The original text states the Recipient *will* return or destroy the information at the direction of the University and will not retain any copies. The changed text states the Recipient *may* at their discretion return or destroy information, and that the Recipient *may* retain any copies. The contradiction is a strong one that would cause confusion and potential unenforceability issues.

perturbation.original_text: No provision of this Agreement will be deemed waived or any breach excused, unless such waiver or consent excusing the breach is in writing and signed by the University.
perturbation.changed_text: Any provision of this Agreement can be waived, and any breach excused, at any time by either party, orally or in writing, without the need for a formal signed document.
perturbation.explanation: Modifying Section 8 to state that either party can waive a provision of the contract, either orally or in writing, contradicts the original text which specified the waiver must be in writing and signed by the University.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. No Waiver. Any provision of this Agreement can be waived, and any breach excused, at any time by either party, orally or in writing, without the need for a formal signed document.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that a waiver can be given orally or in writing by either party without a formal signed document. The original text specifies that waivers must be in writing and signed by the University. This creates a direct conflict in terms of how waivers are permitted, resulting in a strong in-text contradiction. The contradicted text is located in section 8, which is where the original text was changed, meaning the change made to section 8 is now a contradiction with what section 8 used to state.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement contains the entire agreement and understanding of the parties with respect to its subject matter and supersedes all prior proposals, negotiations, agreements, understandings, representations and warranties of any form or nature, whether oral or written, and whether express or implied, which may have been entered into between the parties relating to its subject matter.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement represents the initial understanding of the parties. Subsequent oral agreements and understandings may supplement or modify the terms outlined herein.
perturbation.explanation: Modifying Section 10 to suggest oral agreements can supplement the written agreement directly contradicts the original 'entire agreement' clause, which states the written document supersedes all prior oral agreements. This creates uncertainty about what constitutes the full agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. Entire Agreement and Counterpart. This Agreement represents the initial understanding of the parties. Subsequent oral agreements and understandings may supplement or modify the terms outlined herein.
perturbation.justification: The original text contains an entire agreement clause, which explicitly states that the written agreement supersedes all prior agreements, whether oral or written. The changed text states that subsequent oral agreements may supplement or modify the terms, directly contradicting the original intent of the entire agreement clause. The contradicted text is the same as the Changed Text, showing this contradiction exists within the same section, which makes the agreement unclear and contradicts the original agreement. According to the explanation, it explicitly causes an in-text contradiction.
